error.signature: Cannot find module './my-component' — hyphenated folder misresolved or confused with npm scope

verdict.summary: require('./my-component') works but is confused with npm scoped packages or case-insensitive filesystems create mismatches.

dead_ends.action: Change require to use underscore version
dead_ends.why_fails: If the actual folder has hyphens, changing to underscores creates a mismatch
Adding package.json in every sub-folder creates unnecessary complexity for a naming issue

workarounds.action: Use consistent casing and exact folder name in require/import path
workarounds.how: import MyComponent from './my-component/index.js'  // explicit exact path

workarounds.action: Configure module aliases in tsconfig/webpack to abstract the hyphenated path
